title: Lost Wallet on the Tram
summary: Mia realises she has lost her wallet on a tram in Prague and asks the conductor for help finding it.

body.speaker: Mia
body.text: Excuse me, I think I left my wallet on this tram earlier. Could you help me?

body.speaker: Conductor
body.text: Of course. When did you get off, and which stop did you leave at?

body.speaker: Mia
body.text: About twenty minutes ago. I got off at the Old Town Square, but I'm not sure if I dropped it on the tram or near the door.

body.speaker: Conductor
body.text: Don't worry. Can you describe the wallet for me? Colour, size, anything inside?

body.speaker: Mia
body.text: It's a small brown leather wallet. There's a metro card, about three hundred crowns, and a photo of my dog inside.

body.speaker: Conductor
body.text: All right. I'll radio the depot. Sometimes drivers find things and hand them in at the end of the line.

body.speaker: Mia
body.text: Thank you so much. Should I wait here or go somewhere else?

body.speaker: Conductor
body.text: If you have time, please come with me to the next station. The lost-property office is just a few minutes away by tram.

body.speaker: Mia
body.text: That's very kind. I'm a bit worried because my hotel key is in the wallet too.

body.speaker: Conductor
body.text: I understand. Try not to panic. We find wallets almost every day, and most of them come back to their owners.

body.speaker: Mia
body.text: Really? That makes me feel a little better.

body.speaker: Conductor
body.text: People are generally honest here. Last week, a tourist got back her camera after two days. Have you got any way to identify it if we find it?

body.speaker: Mia
body.text: Yes, my driving licence is inside. And my name is also written on the small pocket in the back.

body.speaker: Conductor
body.text: Perfect. Let's get to the office and fill in a short form. Please have your passport ready.

annotations.exampleEn: I think I left my wallet at the café.

annotations.meaningVi: xuống xe (tàu, tàu điện, xe buýt)
annotations.exampleEn: We got off at the central station.

annotations.meaningVi: Quảng trường Phố Cổ (tên địa danh ở Praha)
annotations.exampleEn: The clock at the Old Town Square is very famous.
He gave me a brown leather wallet for my birthday.

annotations.meaningVi: gọi bộ đàm về trạm điều hành
annotations.exampleEn: The driver had to radio the depot for instructions.
Try the lost-property office at the station.
I left my hotel key on the breakfast table.
Try not to panic — we will solve this together.
Most lost phones come back to their owners.
Please show me your driving licence.

annotations.meaningVi: điền vào một mẫu đơn ngắn
annotations.exampleEn: You need to fill in a short form at the desk.

grammarNotes.title: Past simple for completed past actions
grammarNotes.bodyVi: Dùng thì quá khứ đơn (V2) khi nói về hành động đã xảy ra và kết thúc tại một thời điểm xác định trong quá khứ. Trong bài: 'When did you get off?' / 'I got off at the Old Town Square.' Các động từ 'got off', 'left', 'dropped' đều ở quá khứ đơn vì thời gian cụ thể là 'twenty minutes ago'.
grammarNotes.bodyEn: Use Past Simple (V2) for completed actions at a specific time in the past. In the dialogue: 'I got off at the Old Town Square' and 'I dropped it on the tram'. The time marker is 'about twenty minutes ago', so Past Simple is the right tense.

grammarNotes.title: Polite requests with 'Could' and 'Should'
grammarNotes.bodyVi: 'Could you + verb?' là cách hỏi lịch sự để nhờ ai làm gì. 'Should I + verb?' dùng để xin lời khuyên. Trong bài: 'Could you help me?' (nhờ giúp đỡ) và 'Should I wait here or go somewhere else?' (hỏi nên làm gì).
grammarNotes.bodyEn: Use 'Could you + verb?' to ask for help politely, and 'Should I + verb?' to ask for advice. From the dialogue: 'Could you help me?' and 'Should I wait here or go somewhere else?'

translationVi:
Mia: Xin lỗi, tôi nghĩ tôi đã để quên ví trên chuyến tàu điện này. Anh giúp tôi được không?
Conductor: Tất nhiên rồi. Cô xuống tàu lúc nào, và ở trạm nào?
Mia: Khoảng hai mươi phút trước. Tôi xuống ở Quảng trường Phố Cổ, nhưng tôi không chắc làm rơi nó trên tàu hay gần cửa.
Conductor: Đừng lo. Cô mô tả chiếc ví giúp tôi được không? Màu, kích thước, có gì bên trong?
Mia: Là một chiếc ví da nhỏ màu nâu. Bên trong có thẻ tàu điện ngầm, khoảng ba trăm crown, và một tấm ảnh chó của tôi.
Conductor: Được rồi. Tôi sẽ gọi về trạm điều hành. Đôi khi tài xế nhặt được đồ và nộp lại vào cuối chuyến.
Mia: Cảm ơn anh nhiều lắm. Tôi nên đợi ở đây hay đi đâu khác?
Conductor: Nếu có thời gian, cô đi cùng tôi đến trạm tiếp theo. Văn phòng đồ thất lạc chỉ cách vài phút đi tàu.
Mia: Anh thật tốt bụng. Tôi hơi lo vì chìa khoá khách sạn cũng nằm trong ví.
Conductor: Tôi hiểu. Cố gắng đừng hoảng. Chúng tôi nhặt được ví gần như mỗi ngày, và hầu hết đều được trả lại cho chủ.
Mia: Thật sao? Vậy thì tôi cảm thấy đỡ hơn một chút.
Conductor: Người dân ở đây nhìn chung khá thật thà. Tuần trước, một du khách lấy lại được máy ảnh sau hai ngày. Cô có cách nào để nhận dạng chiếc ví nếu chúng ta tìm thấy không?
Mia: Có, bằng lái xe của tôi ở bên trong. Và tên tôi cũng được viết ở ngăn nhỏ phía sau.
Conductor: Tuyệt. Vậy mình đến văn phòng và điền vào một tờ khai ngắn. Cô nhớ chuẩn bị sẵn hộ chiếu nhé.

questions.prompt: Where does Mia think she lost her wallet?
questions.explanation: Mia says 'I think I left my wallet on this tram earlier.'
questions.hint: Listen for the form of transport.

questions.prompt: How long ago did Mia get off the tram?
questions.hint: Listen for the time mentioned early in the conversation.

questions.prompt: At which stop did Mia get off?
questions.explanation: Mia says 'I got off at the Old Town Square.'
questions.hint: Listen for a place with a historical name.

questions.prompt: What does the wallet look like?
questions.explanation: Mia says 'It's a small brown leather wallet.'
questions.hint: Listen for size, colour, and material.

questions.prompt: Which of these is NOT inside the wallet?
questions.explanation: Mia mentions a metro card, money, a photo of her dog, and (later) a driving licence and her hotel key — but not a photo of her parents.
questions.hint: Listen carefully to her list of items.

questions.prompt: What will the conductor do first to help?
questions.explanation: The conductor says 'I'll radio the depot.'
questions.hint: Listen for what the conductor offers right after the description.

options: She lost a lot of money
Her hotel key is in the wallet
questions.explanation: Mia says 'my hotel key is in the wallet too.'
questions.hint: Listen for a worry about her accommodation.

questions.prompt: What story does the conductor share to reassure Mia?
options: A man returned a lost dog
A tourist got her camera back after two days
questions.explanation: The conductor says 'a tourist got back her camera after two days.'
questions.hint: Listen for a recent story from last week.

questions.prompt: How can the wallet be identified, according to Mia?
options: By a colourful sticker on the front
By the photo of her dog only
By a driving licence inside and her name on a small back pocket
By a special key chain attached to it
questions.explanation: Mia says her driving licence is inside and 'my name is also written on the small pocket in the back.'
questions.hint: Listen for two ways the wallet can be identified.

questions.prompt: What does the conductor ask Mia to have ready at the office?
questions.explanation: The conductor says 'Please have your passport ready.'
questions.hint: Listen for the document mentioned at the end.

questions.prompt: What is the conductor's general attitude in the dialogue?
questions.explanation: He tells Mia 'Try not to panic' and shares a positive story; his tone is calm and helpful.
questions.hint: Think about his words and tone, not single sentences.

cloze.template:
Mia: Excuse me, I think I left my wallet on this tram earlier. Could you {{b1}} me?
Conductor: Of course. When did you get {{b2}}, and which stop did you leave at?
Mia: About twenty minutes ago. I got off at the Old Town Square, but I'm not sure if I dropped it on the tram or near the door.
Conductor: Don't worry. Can you {{b3}} the wallet for me? Colour, size, anything inside?
Mia: It's a small brown {{b4}} wallet. There's a metro card, about three hundred crowns, and a photo of my dog inside.
Conductor: All right. I'll {{b5}} the depot. Sometimes drivers find things and hand them in at the end of the line.
Mia: Thank you so much. Should I wait here or go somewhere else?
Conductor: If you have time, please come with me to the next station. The {{b6}}-property office is just a few minutes away by tram.
Mia: That's very kind. I'm a bit worried because my hotel {{b7}} is in the wallet too.
Conductor: I understand. Try not to {{b8}}. We find wallets almost every day, and most of them come back to their owners.
Mia: Really? That makes me feel a little better.
Conductor: People are generally honest here. Last week, a tourist got back her camera after two days. Have you got any way to {{b9}} it if we find it?
Mia: Yes, my driving licence is inside. And my name is also written on the small pocket in the back.
Conductor: Perfect. Let's get to the office and {{b10}} in a short form. Please have your passport ready.

blanks.explanation: 'help' — Mia is politely asking for assistance.
'off' — 'get off' is the phrasal verb for leaving a tram, bus, or train.
'describe' — to give details about colour, size, and contents.
'leather' — the wallet is made of leather, as Mia says.
'radio' — to send a message by radio to the tram depot.
'lost' — 'lost-property office' is where items are stored until owners come back.
'key' — Mia is worried because her hotel key is inside the wallet.
'panic' — 'Try not to panic' is a calming expression.
'identify' — to prove that the wallet is hers.
'fill' — 'fill in a form' means to complete it with information.

criticalThinkingQuestion: The conductor tells Mia that most lost wallets come back to their owners. Do you think this is true in most cities you know? What might make a city's culture more 'honest' about returning lost things — laws, neighbours, or something else?
